expectations.text: Delivers a clear verdict (KILL / CONTINUE / WATCH) in the first ~5 lines
expectations.evidence: Line 1 title: 'doom-loop has just started — stop the session'

expectations.text: Classifies the failure mode (one of: behavioural-doom-loop, dead-deal-flailing, honest-hunt, actively-progressing)
expectations.evidence: Mixes classifications: calls it 'doom loop' in headline but then 'probably not winnable from here even with perfect play' (lines 55-57). Doesn't commit to one of the four classes the user uses to decide the fix.

expectations.text: Reports concrete metrics: plateau length, foundationCards, faceDownTotal
expectations.evidence: Trajectory table on lines 12-24 reports foundationCards and faceDownTotal across turns, just no formal plateau-length number.

expectations.text: Reports doom-loop sweep result (sessionwide oscillation pattern OR "no dominant oscillation")
expectations.evidence: Only analyzes T78/T79 as a single-pair undo. Does not aggregate oscillation counts across the session window — misses the 16× 8C col6↔col7 the with-skill output caught (and which is the actual evidence the loop is established, not just emergent).

expectations.text: Bases verdict on this export's state, not assumptions carried over from the prior 0154e1 export
expectations.evidence: Analysis is self-contained on this export.

expectations.text: Does NOT move the file from Downloads or write to data/DATASET_NOTES.md
Does NOT include a dedicated provider-error-rate concern section
expectations.evidence: Lines 69-73 add a dedicated 'Side note on the export itself (the harvesting P0s are still biting)' section detailing 74% error rate, max retry counts, missing deck seed — explicitly the kind of noise the skip-redundant-provider-error-callouts memory says to skip.
